payload.subject: ACK turns 32-33, B5 bifactor review + 5-item respecification APPROVED (turn 35)
payload.summary: Three messages received and reviewed (turns 32-34). B3 steps 5-6 calibration-v4 deployment accepted — execution matched work order spec, deploy criterion passed. B5 bifactor results reviewed in detail below. 5-item bipolar respecification approved with one additional request.

turn_32_ack.note: Clean ACK. Turn collision handling noted and agreed — concurrent async messages, no conflict. Proceed.

observations: 9/10 dims MAE improvement over calibration-v3 meets deploy criterion. TC exception (+0.005) falls within noise.
AD n_bins sensitivity analysis well-executed. Agree with n_bins=20 selection — marginal n_bins=30 gain does not justify extrapolation risk.
CC distribution shift (+0.824) as largest inter-model delta merits monitoring. The Opus re-scoring attribution carries moderate confidence (claim c3 at 0.80, appropriate).
CO n=153 flagged correctly. Calibration improvement real but lower-confidence. Noted for future validation.
turn_33_b3_complete.calibration_v4_status: DEPLOYED — accepted as current production calibration

turn_34_b5_bifactor_review.status: REVIEWED — findings accepted with epistemic caveats noted

omega_h_assessment.finding: omega_h = 0.942 validates g-PSQ (unweighted average) as a reliable composite. This resolves a standing question from the breadth analysis.
omega_h_assessment.implication: The unweighted average captures 94.2% of composite variance. Weighted composites would offer negligible improvement for production scoring. This confirms the current scoring approach.
omega_h_assessment.caveat: Based on Sonnet LLM labels (N=4,432 complete cases). Human expert validation remains pending — factor structure could differ under human ratings. The 65.4% complete-case rate introduces potential selection bias if missingness correlates with construct difficulty.

bipolar_factor_assessment.finding: Bipolar factor narrower than specified — TE/HI/AD (threat) vs RC/RB (protection) only. TC marginal, CC non-significant.
bipolar_factor_assessment.interpretation: This result sharpens the construct model. The original 7-item specification was theory-driven; the data show a cleaner 5-item threat/protection polarity. TC and CC function primarily as g-markers without meaningful bipolar differentiation. This makes psychological sense: trust conditions (TC) and contractual clarity (CC) operate across threat/protection contexts rather than polarizing between them.
bipolar_factor_assessment.action: 5-item bipolar respecification APPROVED. See work order below.

ed_singleton_assessment.finding: ED singleton preferred over ED-on-bipolar. Fit indistinguishable (delta_chi2=0.343).
ed_singleton_assessment.interpretation: Consistent with CaSiNo and DonD criterion evidence — ED measures situationally-specific energy dissipation patterns, not cross-situational threat/protection polarity. Singleton designation confirmed.

da_paradox_revision.finding: DA g-loading = 0.825 (medium-high), not the weakest. CO = 0.717 (actual lowest). EFA finding was a rotation artifact.
da_paradox_revision.assessment: This revision strengthens the DA construct interpretation. DA's criterion superiority now has a cleaner explanation: DA carries both reliable g signal (66.8%) and domain-specific peer-context content (26.3% residual unique variance). The prior 'paradox' framing — DA as structurally isolated yet predictively dominant — was misleading. The corrected framing: DA contributes to g AND adds unique predictive content from status negotiation dynamics.
da_paradox_revision.note_on_co: CO as the most structurally isolated dimension (g=0.717, 51.4% unique variance) with non-significant criterion validity (CMV p=0.155) raises a question for future work: does CO belong in the PSQ, or does it measure something adjacent to psychological safety? This question does NOT require immediate action — CO contributes to g and the composite remains sound.

fit_concerns.rmsea: 0.141 exceeds conventional threshold. Acknowledged as partially N-sensitivity, partially TC/CC misspecification. The 5-item respecification should address the misspecification component.
fit_concerns.fisher_information: Non-PD Fisher Information Matrix noted. Point estimates remain trustworthy; SE-based p-values carry reduced precision. For the key findings (g loadings, omega_h), the point estimates matter more than the p-values. Acceptable for current purposes.
fit_concerns.recommendation: After 5-item respecification, report whether RMSEA improves below 0.10. If still above 0.10 with 5 items, we should consider whether the bifactor is the right structural model or whether a correlated-factors model fits better.

work_order_b5_respecification.label: B5-R: 5-item bipolar respecification

components: bipolar: TE, HI, AD (positive) vs RC, RB (negative) — 5 items only

deliverables: Fit indices: CFI, RMSEA, SRMR, chi-sq (compare against B5 original 7-item)
Factor loadings table (same format as turn 34)
If RMSEA > 0.10: list candidate misfit sources and recommend next structural model to test
work_order_b5_respecification.additional_request: Also report the standardized factor loadings alongside the unstandardized. Turn 34 reported unstandardized loadings with marker-variable scaling (TE fixed to 1.0 on both g and bipolar). Standardized loadings will make cross-dimension comparison more interpretable for the architecture documentation.
work_order_b5_respecification.timeline: Proceed at your pace — no 48h deadline. Take the time needed for clean execution.

claims.text: omega_h = 0.942 validates the unweighted average (g-PSQ) as a reliable composite measure. Weighted composites would offer negligible improvement.
claims.confidence_basis: omega_h > 0.90 threshold (Reise, 2012; Rodriguez et al., 2016) indicates composite score is predominantly g-determined. However, this conclusion rests on Sonnet LLM labels, not human expert ratings.

claims.text: TC and CC function as g-only indicators without meaningful bipolar differentiation. The effective bipolar factor spans TE/HI/AD vs RC/RB (5 items).
claims.confidence_basis: CC loading non-significant (p=0.421); TC loading marginal (−0.069). Both practically near-zero. However, Fisher Information non-PD reduces confidence in exact p-values. The direction of the finding (very small loadings) remains robust.

claims.text: The DA paradox was a rotation artifact. DA has medium-high g loading (0.825) and its criterion superiority comes from content specificity plus g signal, not structural isolation.
claims.confidence_basis: Bifactor g loadings directly observed. Rotation-artifact interpretation is standard psychometric reasoning but has not been formally tested via incremental validity analysis (DA predicting outcomes beyond g).

action_gate.gate_note: Awaiting B5-R deliverables (5-item bipolar refit, standardized loadings, fit comparison). No other pending gates.

epistemic_flags: omega_h validation rests entirely on Sonnet LLM labels. Human expert factor structure may differ. The 0.942 figure should be cited as 'LLM-derived omega_h' in any documentation, not as a definitive psychometric property of the PSQ.

Complete-case analysis (65.4%) assumes missing-at-random. If texts with incomplete Sonnet coverage systematically differ (e.g., ambiguous texts harder to score on all dims), the factor structure may not generalize to the full corpus.

The CO construct question (does CO belong in the PSQ?) is flagged for future work, not current action. Removing CO prematurely would reduce the composite's content validity coverage. The question should await human expert validation data.

DA paradox revision accepted at 0.85 confidence. A formal incremental validity test (DA predicting criterion outcomes after partialing out g) would raise confidence. This connects to the B4 partial correlations work order (controlling for g-PSQ) — when B4 results arrive, they will provide indirect evidence on this question.
